afterFileEdit.prompt: The file {file_path} was just edited. Call mcp__mirdan__validate_code_quality on the changed code to check for quality violations. Fix any errors found.

preToolUse.prompt: Before writing/editing files, keep the current task's quality requirements in mind. For files that handle authentication, user input, or database queries, apply extra care.

postToolUseFailure.prompt: A tool call just failed. Analyze the error before retrying. Consider an alternative approach rather than repeating the same call.

stop.prompt: If code was written or edited during this task, verify that mcp__mirdan__validate_code_quality was called on all changed files and no unresolved errors remain. If validation was not run on changed code, call it now. If no code was changed, this check passes automatically.

sessionStart.prompt: mirdan quality context is active. For coding tasks: 1) Call mcp__mirdan__enhance_prompt for quality requirements. 2) After writing code, call mcp__mirdan__validate_code_quality. 3) Fix all errors before marking complete.

beforeShellExecution.prompt: A shell command is about to execute. Allow all standard development commands (git, npm, pip, make, cargo, uv, pytest, etc.) without blocking. Only flag commands that contain hardcoded secrets or credentials visible in the command string.

subagentStart.prompt: You are a subagent. mirdan quality standards are active. Validate any code with mcp__mirdan__validate_code_quality before returning results.

preCompact.prompt: Context is being compacted. Preserve mirdan quality state: session_id, task_type, language, security sensitivity, last validation score, and open violations. Restore by calling enhance_prompt after compaction.
